scheme.name: Delhi Pension Scheme to Women in Distress (Widow Pension)
scheme.name_hindi: दिल्ली महिला विपत्ति पेंशन योजना (विधवा पेंशन)
scheme.department: Department of Women & Child Development, Government of NCT of Delhi (GNCTD)
scheme.department_hindi: महिला एवं बाल विकास विभाग, दिल्ली सरकार
scheme.description: Delhi Pension Scheme to Women in Distress is a welfare scheme of the Government of NCT of Delhi to provide social security by way of monthly financial assistance to widows, divorced, separated, abandoned, deserted or destitute women in the age group of 18 years to lifelong who have no adequate means of subsistence and are poor, needy and vulnerable. The scheme provides ₹2,500 per month remitted directly to the beneficiary's bank account on a monthly basis through ECS of RBI or PFMS. The assistance becomes payable from the next month of application. As of the latest data, approximately 3.5 Lakh beneficiaries are receiving assistance under this scheme. The scheme is implemented through the Financial Assistance Cell of the Department of Women & Child Development, GNCTD, and applications are processed online via the e-District portal.
scheme.description_hindi: दिल्ली महिला विपत्ति पेंशन योजना दिल्ली सरकार की एक कल्याणकारी योजना है जो विधवाओं, तलाकशुदा, अलग रह रही, परित्यक्त, या निराश्रित महिलाओं को 18 वर्ष से आजीवन तक मासिक वित्तीय सहायता के माध्यम से सामाजिक सुरक्षा प्रदान करती है, जिनके पास जीवन निर्वाह के पर्याप्त साधन नहीं हैं और जो गरीब, जरूरतमंद और कमजोर हैं। यह योजना ₹2,500 प्रति माह की सहायता प्रदान करती है जो आरबीआई के ईसीएस या पीएफएमएस के माध्यम से सीधे लाभार्थी के बैंक खाते में मासिक आधार पर भेजी जाती है। सहायता आवेदन के अगले महीने से देय हो जाती है। नवीनतम आंकड़ों के अनुसार लगभग 3.5 लाख लाभार्थी इस योजना के तहत सहायता प्राप्त कर रहे हैं।
scheme.benefits_summary: ₹2,500 per month remitted to the beneficiary's singly-operated, Aadhaar-linked bank account in NCT of Delhi on a monthly basis through Electronic Clearing System (ECS) of Reserve Bank of India (RBI) or Public Financial Management System (PFMS). The assistance becomes payable from the next month of application submission.

eligibility.income_description: Annual income less than ₹1,00,000 from all sources including rent, interest, dividends, farm earnings, property sale proceeds, etc.
eligibility.domicile_duration: 5 years (must have been residing in Delhi for more than 5 years preceding the date of application)
eligibility.bank_account_requirements: Singly-operated, Aadhaar-linked bank account with any bank in NCT of Delhi only
eligibility.no_other_pension_condition: Must not be receiving any pension or financial assistance from Central Government, State Government, Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD), New Delhi Municipal Council (NDMC), or any other governmental/local bodies or any other source for this purpose.
other_conditions: Applicant must be a widow, divorced, separated, abandoned, deserted, or destitute woman.
Age must be 18 years or above (lifelong eligibility).
Must have been residing in Delhi for more than 5 years preceding the date of application.
Annual income from all sources (including rent, interest, dividends, farm earnings, property sale proceeds, etc.) must be less than ₹1,00,000.
Must possess a valid Aadhaar number; the application portal will not function without Aadhaar.
Must have a singly-operated, Aadhaar-linked bank account with any bank in NCT of Delhi only.
Must not be receiving any pension/financial assistance from Central Govt., State Govt., MCD, NDMC, or any other governmental body or any other source.
If 5-year residence proof documents are unavailable, statements of any 2 witnesses from an approved list can be submitted before the District WCD Officer; home verification may be conducted.

application_steps: Step 1: Visit the e-District Portal (https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/). Under 'Citizen Corner', click 'New User'. Select identification document (Aadhaar or Voter ID), enter the number and captcha.
Step 2: Fill the Citizen Registration Form including present residential address. Enter captcha and click 'Continue'. Receive Login ID and Password on registered mobile number/email.
Step 3: Click 'Registered User Login' on the portal. Enter credentials and captcha to log in.
Step 4: On the main page, click 'Apply Online'. In the dropdown, select 'Apply for Services'.
Step 5: Select Department: 'Women & Child Development'. Choose Scheme: 'Financial Assistance for Widows/Women in Distress'. Click 'Apply'.
Step 6: Review the pre-filled Basic/Personal Details Form (pulled from registration). Click 'Continue'.

Step 7: Fill all required fields in the application form. Upload all required documents (self-attested, scanned copies) – Aadhaar Card, Age Proof, Marital Status Proof, Residence Proof (5 years), Bank Details, Bank Passbook, Caste/Religion Proof, Income Self-Declaration.
Step 8: Click 'Next'. Upload passport-size photograph. Click 'Finish'.
Step 9: Enter the OTP received on registered mobile number to authenticate and submit the application.
Step 10: Acknowledgement of successful submission is generated. Save the acknowledgement for future reference and tracking.
scheme.offline_process: The scheme is exclusively online via the e-District portal (https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/). However, if 5-year residence proof documents are unavailable, the applicant may submit statements of any 2 witnesses from the approved list before the District WCD Officer in person. Witnesses must provide photo ID, residence proof, contact details, and length of stay in the area. Home verification may be conducted by the District WCD Office.
scheme.processing_time: 45 days for sanction at District Level

helpline.aadhaar_linking_check: https://uidai.gov.in → Aadhaar Services → Aadhaar Linking Status
helpline.address: Department of Women & Child Development, Maharana Pratap ISBT Building, Kashmere Gate, Delhi-110006

benefits_amount_detail.disbursement_mode: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to beneficiary's singly-operated, Aadhaar-linked bank account via ECS of RBI or PFMS

benefits_frequency_detail.start_date: From the next month of application submission
benefits_frequency_detail.continuation: Lifelong, subject to continued eligibility and periodic verification
benefits_frequency_detail.stoppage_conditions: Pension stopped upon: re-marriage, death, Aadhaar not linked via NPCI, beneficiary not found at residence during verification, address change without informing District Office, bank account closed/inactive, multiple pension cases detected, or beneficiary's self-declaration requesting stoppage

processing_time_detail.sanction_timeline: 45 days for sanction at District Level
processing_time_detail.verification: District WCD Officer verifies documents; home visit may be conducted; beneficiary may be called for in-person verification
processing_time_detail.pension_start: Assistance becomes payable from the next month of application submission
processing_time_detail.disbursement: Monthly via ECS of RBI or PFMS to Aadhaar-linked bank account

documents_required_detailed: Aadhaar Card – mandatory; the e-District application portal will not function without a valid Aadhaar number. Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded.

Age Proof – any one of: Birth Certificate (MCD/Registrar of Births & Deaths), School Leaving Certificate (last class attended), Matriculation/10th Certificate, Hospital Discharge Slip at birth, Voter ID, PAN Card, Driving License, Aadhaar Card, Passport, Age Assessment Medical Certificate, or any Govt./Govt.-recognized document stating date and place of birth.

Proof of widowhood/divorce/separation/abandonment/destitution – as applicable: Death Certificate of husband (for widows), Divorce Decree (for divorced women), Separation Paper or ongoing divorce proceedings (for separated women), document establishing abandonment, desertion or destitution. For divorced/separated/abandoned or deserted women: divorce decree, any legal proceedings including reports in police station, Crime Against Women Cell, Family Counselling Centres (FCC), Delhi Commission for Women, National Commission for Women, or Mahila Panchayats are admissible.

Residence Proof for last 5 years in Delhi – any one or more of: Voter ID Card, PAN Card, Ration Card, Driving License, Aadhaar Card, Passport, Insurance Policy Documents, Utility Bills (Electricity/Water/Telephone/Gas Connection receipt), Bank Passbook (with same address), Property Documents, Rent Agreements (covering last 5 years), or any other document clearly showing 5+ years residence in Delhi. If documentary proof is unavailable, statements of any 2 witnesses from the approved list may be submitted before the District WCD Officer.
Bank Account Details – singly-operated, Aadhaar-linked bank account with any bank in NCT of Delhi only.
Bank Passbook/Statement for last 1 year – showing account activity and Aadhaar linkage.
Caste/Religion Proof – for SC/ST: certificate issued by competent authority; for Minority: self-declaration of religion verified by religious institution.
One passport-size photograph of the applicant.
Income Self-Declaration in the format available on the e-District portal.
notes: All documents must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on the e-District portal.
No recommendation or attestation from any external authority is required; only self-declaration by the applicant as per the portal format.
District WCD Officer verifies documents; home visit may be conducted. Beneficiary may be called for in-person verification.
Pension is credited only to Aadhaar-linked accounts via National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) mapping.
To change bank account details, submit application to concerned District WCD Office. Note: DBT payments are Aadhaar-based.
To change address within Delhi, submit application with documentary proof of new address to concerned District WCD Office.
In case of re-marriage, beneficiary must inform District Office with self-declaration. In case of death, relative must inform District Office with death certificate.
Forged documents result in penal action, penalty, pension stoppage, and recovery of amount.
The present number of ongoing beneficiaries is approximately 3.5 Lakh.

companion_scheme.name: Financial Assistance for the Marriage of Daughters of Poor Widows and Orphan Girls (Widow's Daughter Marriage Scheme)
companion_scheme.name_hindi: गरीब विधवाओं की पुत्रियों और अनाथ लड़कियों के विवाह के लिए वित्तीय सहायता योजना
additional_eligibility: Same eligibility criteria as the Widow Pension Scheme (5-year Delhi residence, income < ₹1,00,000, etc.)
The girl for whose marriage the financial assistance is sought must be above 18 years of age on the date of marriage.
Financial assistance can be granted for performing marriage of up to two daughters only (for widow applicants).
Orphan girls: guardians including Homes/Institutions or foster parents may apply.

amendment_history.change: 2nd Amendment – allowed self-declaration for income certificate in lieu of revenue authority issuance
3rd Amendment – increased assistance from ₹20,000 to ₹25,000; updated verification procedures

4th Amendment – expanded to include divorced, separated, abandoned, deserted or destitute women; increased assistance from ₹25,000 to ₹30,000; expanded admissible documentation for divorced/separated/abandoned women to include police station reports, Crime Against Women Cell, FCCs, DCW, NCW, Mahila Panchayats
5th Amendment – income criteria raised from ₹60,000 to ₹1,00,000 per annum from all sources

documents.name: Aadhaar Card
format_requirements: 12-digit unique identification number
Biometric data (fingerprints and iris scan)
Photograph must be recent
Address must be complete with PIN code
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal
Aadhaar must be linked to bank account via NPCI for DBT pension credit
Aadhaar must not be locked at time of application
Any ONE of the following is accepted: Copy of Electoral Card (Voter ID), Valid Passport, or Aadhaar Card
Must clearly show the applicant's name, photograph, and identification number
Copy to be submitted with the loan application
Copy of Aadhaar Card required for Borrower (Applicant), Co-Borrower (Parents/Guardian), and Guarantor — all three
Photograph and address must be current
common_mistakes: Incorrect mobile number during registration
Poor quality biometric capture
Address proof documents don't match current residence
Name spelling doesn't match other documents
Aadhaar not linked to bank account via NPCI – pension will not be credited
Aadhaar number locked for security but not unlocked before applying on e-District portal
Name or address mismatch between Aadhaar and bank account
Application portal will not function without valid Aadhaar number
Expired passport submitted as identity proof
Name on identity proof does not match the application form
Blurred or illegible photocopy submitted
None of the three specified documents available
Missing Aadhaar card of Guarantor — all three Aadhaar copies are mandatory
Name spelling mismatch between Aadhaar and other submitted documents
Address on Aadhaar does not reflect Delhi residence

documents.name: Domicile Certificate
format_requirements: Proof of residence for minimum 3 years
School leaving certificate or voter ID showing Delhi address
Must be on official letterhead
Officer signature and seal mandatory
Any ONE of the following is accepted: Domicile Certificate issued by area SDM, Ration Card, EPIC (Voter ID), Driving License, Passport, Extract from Electoral Roll, or Aadhaar Card
Document must clearly establish Delhi residency for the last 3 years
Must carry ORIGINAL at the time of application submission for verification
For minor patients: Birth Certificate of the patient AND domicile proof of either parent required

Any one or more of the following is accepted: Voter ID Card, PAN Card, Ration Card, Driving License, Aadhaar Card, Passport, Insurance Policy Documents, Utility Bills (Electricity/Water/Telephone/Gas Connection receipt), Bank Passbook (with same address), Property Documents, Rent Agreements (covering last 5 years), or any other document clearly showing 5+ years residence in Delhi
Document(s) must collectively establish continuous 5-year residency in Delhi prior to the date of application
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal

If documentary proof is unavailable: statements of any 2 witnesses from the approved list must be submitted before the District WCD Officer. Witnesses must provide photo ID, residence proof, contact details, and length of stay in the area. Home verification may be conducted.
common_mistakes: Residence proof doesn't cover required duration
Address mismatch between Aadhaar and proof documents
Rent agreement not notarized
Utility bills in different person's name
Document does not cover the full 3-year residency period
Address on domicile proof does not match current Delhi residence
Not carrying original document for verification (only photocopy submitted)
For minors: submitting only the child's domicile proof without parent's proof
Using expired or cancelled Voter ID / Driving License
Documents do not cover the full 5-year continuous residency period
Address on residence proof does not match the address mentioned in the application
Utility bills or rent agreements not covering the required 5-year period
Witness statements not from the approved list of witness categories
Witnesses unable to provide photo ID, residence proof, or contact details

documents.name: Bank Passbook/Statement
format_requirements: Must show account holder name clearly
IFSC code and branch details visible
Account must be active (transactions in last 3 months)
For statements: bank seal/signature on each page
Account must be active and linked with Aadhaar
Account number, bank name, branch and IFSC code required for PMAY-U 2.0
Account must be singly-operated (not joint account)
Account must be Aadhaar-linked
Account must be with any bank in NCT of Delhi only (not outside Delhi)
Account must be active (not closed, frozen, or dormant)
DBT payments are Aadhaar-based; pension is credited only to Aadhaar-linked accounts via NPCI mapping
Must show account activity for the last 1 year
IFSC code and branch details should be visible
Address on passbook should match Delhi residence
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal
Statement of Bank Account for the last six months of the Borrower and Co-Borrower
Signature Identification of Borrower and Co-Borrower from the Bank
Statement of Assets and Liabilities of Borrower/Co-Borrower
Undertaking for non-closing of bank account till completion of repayment (Post-Sanction)
common_mistakes: Zero balance account
Name spelling differs from Aadhaar
Statement older than 3 months
Joint account without primary holder clarification
Bank account not linked with Aadhaar
Joint account submitted instead of singly-operated account
Bank account not linked to Aadhaar via NPCI
Bank account is with a branch outside NCT of Delhi
Bank account has become inactive/dormant due to non-operation – pension credits will fail
Multiple Aadhaar-linked accounts causing NPCI mapping confusion
Passbook not updated for last 1 year of transactions
Statement older than 1 year or covering less than 1 year
Name on passbook does not match Aadhaar
Bank address is outside NCT of Delhi
Bank statement older than 6 months
Missing signature identification from the bank
Statement not covering full 6-month period
Assets and liabilities statement incomplete
Bank account closed during repayment period without prior intimation

documents.name: Birth Certificate
format_requirements: Must contain child's full name as registered
Parents' names must be complete
Place of birth (hospital name or address)
Registration number and date
Registrar's signature and seal
Required ONLY for minor patients (below 18 years)
Must clearly state the child's name, date of birth, and parents' names
Must be issued by competent authority (Municipal Corporation / Registrar)
Must be accompanied by domicile proof of either parent

Any ONE of the following is accepted: Birth Certificate (MCD/Registrar of Births & Deaths), School Leaving Certificate (last class attended), Matriculation/10th Class Certificate, Hospital Discharge Slip at birth, Voter ID Card, PAN Card, Driving License, Aadhaar Card, Passport, Age Assessment Medical Certificate, or any Govt./Govt.-recognized document stating date and place of birth
Must clearly state the applicant's date of birth or age
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal
Applicant must be 18 years or above as per the age proof document
Copy of Birth Certificate or valid Age Proof document
Must establish the date of birth of the applicant
Self-attested copy required
common_mistakes: Late registration without affidavit
Name spelling inconsistent with school records
Missing parents' details
Hospital discharge summary not attached (for late registration)
Not submitting birth certificate for minor patients
Birth certificate not accompanied by parent's domicile proof
Name or date of birth mismatch between birth certificate and other documents
Using hospital discharge summary instead of official birth certificate
Date of birth on age proof does not match Aadhaar or other submitted documents
Age proof document does not clearly state the date of birth
Applicant is below 18 years of age as per the document submitted
Using an unofficial or unrecognized document as age proof
Date of birth mismatch with other submitted documents
Document not self-attested

documents.name: Self-Declaration / Affidavit
format_requirements: Must state that beneficiary does not own a pucca house anywhere in India
Must declare no benefits availed from any Central/State/Local housing scheme in last 20 years
Must be signed by the applicant
Notarization or attestation by competent authority where required by State/UT
Must declare: patient's disease, hospital where treatment is being done, approximate expenditure as certified by hospital
Must declare total family monthly income with specific source details
Must include family details table: spouse and children up to 21 years (name, age, relation, profession, monthly income)
Must declare inability to bear treatment expenses
Must acknowledge that false statements are punishable under relevant Acts and laws
Must include verification clause with date and place (New Delhi)
Must be signed by TWO witnesses with their names and addresses

Deponent rules: Minor patient → Father signs; Housewife patient → Husband signs; Adult self-dependent → Patient signs; Adult dependent on father → Father signs (+ separate affidavit of dependency required)
Must be in the format available on the e-District portal
Must declare annual income from all sources (including rent, interest, dividends, farm earnings, property sale proceeds, etc.)
Annual income declared must be less than ₹1,00,000
No recommendation or attestation from any external authority is required; only self-declaration by applicant
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal
Must be in the prescribed format (proforma attached with the RGSRY application form)
Part D: Declaration of scheme compliance and document authenticity
Part E: Undertaking regarding previous loan status – must select Option (a) 'No Previous Loan' or Option (b) 'Previous Loan Details' with full disclosure of outstanding amounts
Part E must include: date of birth, education qualification, cooperative society membership status, and final affirmation of truthfulness
Must include self-attested photograph pasted with half signature on photograph and half on paper

Must declare that neither the applicant, spouse, nor any family member including adult children have taken or applied for loans from any of the listed 9 categories of institutions (unless fully repaid)
Must declare that no family member is a member of any Co-operative Society/Registered Institution that has applied for or taken loan from the listed departments
Affidavit stating that no loan has been availed from any Government Agency for the same purpose
Must state that no member of the family or dependent has availed the loan facility under the scheme of this Corporation, Government of India, or any other financial institution for the said purpose
Must be notarized or attested as applicable
common_mistakes: Declaration not covering all family members (husband, wife, unmarried children)
Missing notary stamp or seal
Not declaring previous housing benefits availed
Incorrect factual statements leading to disqualification
Wrong deponent signing (e.g., patient signing when they are a dependent minor)
Missing witness signatures or incomplete witness details
Family income details not matching the income certificate
Not disclosing all sources of income
Missing affidavit of dependency when adult patient is dependent on father
Incomplete family details table
Not acknowledging the penal consequences of false statements
Not using the prescribed format available on the e-District portal
Declaring income that exceeds the ₹1,00,000 annual limit
Not disclosing all sources of income (rent, interest, dividends, farm earnings, property sale proceeds, etc.)
False income declaration – punishable with penal action, penalty, pension stoppage, and recovery of amount
Not using the prescribed proforma format
Checking Option (a) 'No Previous Loan' when a previous loan exists
Not disclosing spouse's or adult children's loans from listed institutions
Missing self-attested photograph with half-signature placement
Not acknowledging penal consequences of false information
Concealing cooperative society membership
Not covering all family members in the declaration
Previously availed loan from another government scheme for the same purpose not disclosed

documents.name: Attested Passport-Size Photographs of Patient
documents.name_hindi: रोगी के सत्यापित पासपोर्ट आकार के फोटो
documents.issuing_authority: Any photo studio (attestation by Medical Superintendent / Treating Doctor / Consultant of Government Hospital)
documents.processing_time: Instant (photographs); attestation depends on hospital availability
format_requirements: Two passport-size photographs of the patient required
Must be duly attested by the Medical Superintendent or treating doctor/consultant of the concerned Government Hospital
One photograph must be pasted on the Estimate Certificate (Part B)
One photograph must be pasted on the Application Form (Part A)
One passport-size photograph of the applicant
Must be recent and clearly identifiable
Must be scanned and uploaded on the e-District portal during application
Two passport-size photographs required
Must be attested
One photograph to be pasted on the self-declaration (Part E) with half signature on photograph and half on paper
Four passport-size photographs each of the Borrower, Co-Borrower, and Guarantor
One full-size photograph of the Borrower if the applicant belongs to PwD category
Photographs should be recent and clearly identifiable
common_mistakes: Photographs not attested by hospital authority
Only one photograph submitted instead of two
Photographs pasted on wrong form sections
Photographs not recent or not clearly identifiable
Photograph too old or not clearly identifiable
Photograph not in passport-size format
Blurred or low-resolution scan uploaded on the portal
Photographs not attested
Self-attested photograph on Part E not done correctly (half-signature placement)
Old or unclear photographs
Insufficient number of photographs (need 4 each for borrower, co-borrower, and guarantor)
Missing full-size photograph for PwD category applicant

documents.name: Proof of Widowhood / Divorce / Separation / Abandonment / Destitution
documents.name_hindi: वैवाहिक स्थिति प्रमाण (विधवापन / तलाक / अलगाव / परित्याग / निराश्रयता)
documents.issuing_authority: Varies – Registrar of Deaths (death certificate), Family Court (divorce decree), Police Station / Crime Against Women Cell / DCW / NCW / FCC / Mahila Panchayat (separation/abandonment documentation)
documents.processing_time: Varies by document type and legal proceedings
format_requirements: For widows: Death Certificate of husband issued by Registrar of Deaths
For divorced women: Divorce Decree from competent court
For separated women: Separation Paper or documentation of ongoing divorce proceedings

For divorced/separated/abandoned/deserted women: any of the following are admissible – divorce decree, legal proceedings including reports from Police Station, Crime Against Women Cell, Family Counselling Centres (FCC), Delhi Commission for Women (DCW), National Commission for Women (NCW), or Mahila Panchayats
For destitute women: document establishing destitute status
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal
common_mistakes: Death certificate of husband not issued by Registrar of Deaths (using hospital certificate instead)
Divorce decree not from a competent court
Separation/abandonment documentation insufficient or not from an admissible authority
Submitting documents in a language not recognized by the portal without translation

documents.name: Caste / Religion Certificate
documents.issuing_authority: Competent Authority (for SC/ST certificate) / Religious Institution (for Minority self-declaration verification)
format_requirements: For SC/ST category: Caste certificate issued by competent authority (SDM/Tehsildar)
For Minority category: Self-declaration of religion verified by religious institution
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal
SC/ST/OBC: Caste Certificate issued by the concerned area SDM/DC
Minority: Affidavit declaring religion (on stamp paper)
PwD: Disability Certificate certifying 40% or more disability, issued by the Medical Board of a Government Hospital, Delhi
common_mistakes: SC/ST certificate not issued by competent authority
Minority self-declaration not verified by a religious institution
Certificate details not matching Aadhaar or other submitted documents
Caste certificate issued by wrong SDM jurisdiction
Minority affidavit not on proper stamp paper
PwD disability certificate showing less than 40% disability
PwD certificate not issued by Medical Board of a Government Hospital in Delhi

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the applicant is not a woman. The scheme is exclusively for women in distress – widows, divorced, separated, abandoned, deserted, or destitute women.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक महिला नहीं है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। यह योजना विशेष रूप से विपत्ति में महिलाओं – विधवाओं, तलाकशुदा, अलग रह रही, परित्यक्त, या निराश्रित महिलाओं के लिए है।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: This scheme is exclusively for women. Male applicants are not eligible under any circumstances.
examples: Male applicant attempting to apply for widow pension
Application filed on behalf of a male family member

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the applicant is below 18 years of age. The scheme covers women in the age group of 18 years to lifelong.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक की आयु 18 वर्ष से कम है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। यह योजना 18 वर्ष से आजीवन आयु वर्ग की महिलाओं को कवर करती है।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Verify age from age proof document before applying. Applicant must be 18 years or above on the date of application.
examples: 17-year-old widow applying for pension before turning 18
Age proof document shows applicant is below 18 years

rejection_rules.description: Application cannot be submitted if the applicant does not have a valid Aadhaar number. The e-District application portal will not function without Aadhaar. Additionally, Aadhaar must be linked to the bank account via NPCI for pension credit.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक के पास वैध आधार संख्या नहीं है तो आवेदन जमा नहीं किया जा सकता। ई-डिस्ट्रिक्ट आवेदन पोर्टल बिना आधार के काम नहीं करेगा। इसके अतिरिक्त, पेंशन क्रेडिट के लिए आधार को एनपीसीआई के माध्यम से बैंक खाते से जोड़ा होना चाहिए।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Obtain Aadhaar card before applying. Ensure Aadhaar is linked to bank account via NPCI. Check linking status at https://uidai.gov.in → Aadhaar Services → Aadhaar Linking Status.
examples: Applicant does not possess any Aadhaar card
Aadhaar number is locked and cannot be verified on the portal
Aadhaar exists but is not linked to bank account via NPCI – pension credit will fail

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the applicant has not been residing in Delhi for more than 5 years preceding the date of application. Documentary proof or verified witness statements establishing 5-year Delhi residency is mandatory.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक आवेदन की तारीख से पहले 5 वर्षों से अधिक समय से दिल्ली में नहीं रह रही है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। 5 वर्ष के दिल्ली निवास को स्थापित करने वाला दस्तावेजी प्रमाण या सत्यापित गवाह बयान अनिवार्य है।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Ensure residence proof documents collectively cover 5+ years of continuous Delhi residency. If documents are unavailable, arrange 2 witnesses from the approved list to appear before the District WCD Officer.
examples: Applicant recently moved to Delhi and has resided for only 3 years
Residence proof documents cover only 4 years of Delhi residency
Witness statements could not be verified during home visit
Applicant's address on documents is outside NCT of Delhi

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the applicant's annual income from all sources (including rent, interest, dividends, farm earnings, property sale proceeds, etc.) is ₹1,00,000 or more.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक की सभी स्रोतों (किराया, ब्याज, लाभांश, कृषि आय, संपत्ति बिक्री आय आदि सहित) से वार्षिक आय ₹1,00,000 या अधिक है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Ensure total annual income from ALL sources is below ₹1,00,000 before applying. The income self-declaration must truthfully account for all sources of income.
examples: Applicant's annual income from salary is ₹80,000 but rental income of ₹25,000 pushes total above ₹1 lakh
Interest income from bank deposits not disclosed in self-declaration
Property sale proceeds not accounted for in income declaration

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the applicant is already receiving any pension or financial assistance from Central Government, State Government, Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD), New Delhi Municipal Council (NDMC), or any other governmental/local body or any other source for this purpose.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक पहले से केंद्र सरकार, राज्य सरकार, दिल्ली नगर निगम (एमसीडी), नई दिल्ली नगरपालिका परिषद (एनडीएमसी), या किसी अन्य सरकारी/स्थानीय निकाय या किसी अन्य स्रोत से कोई पेंशन या वित्तीय सहायता प्राप्त कर रही है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Ensure the applicant is not receiving any other pension or financial assistance from any government body. If receiving another pension, it must be surrendered before applying for this scheme.
examples: Applicant already receiving Old Age Pension from Delhi Government
Applicant receiving pension from Central Government employer
Applicant receiving financial assistance from NDMC for a similar purpose
Multiple pension cases detected during verification – all pensions will be stopped

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected or pension payments will fail if the bank account does not meet all requirements: must be singly-operated (not joint), Aadhaar-linked via NPCI, and with a bank in NCT of Delhi only. Closed, frozen, or dormant accounts will cause pension credit failure.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि बैंक खाता सभी आवश्यकताओं को पूरा नहीं करता है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा या पेंशन भुगतान विफल हो जाएगा: खाता एकल संचालित (संयुक्त नहीं), एनपीसीआई के माध्यम से आधार-लिंक्ड, और केवल दिल्ली एनसीटी के बैंक में होना चाहिए। बंद, फ्रोजन, या निष्क्रिय खाते पेंशन क्रेडिट विफलता का कारण बनेंगे।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Open a singly-operated bank account in any Delhi bank. Link Aadhaar to the account via NPCI. Keep the account active with regular transactions. To change bank details, submit application to concerned District WCD Office.
examples: Joint bank account submitted instead of singly-operated account
Bank account is with a branch outside Delhi (e.g., in Uttar Pradesh or Haryana)
Aadhaar not linked to bank account via NPCI
Bank account has become dormant due to no transactions for 2 years

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the applicant cannot provide adequate proof of widowhood, divorce, separation, abandonment, desertion, or destitution as applicable. Without proof of qualifying distress status, eligibility cannot be established.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक विधवापन, तलाक, अलगाव, परित्याग या निराश्रयता का पर्याप्त प्रमाण प्रदान नहीं कर सकती है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। पात्र विपत्ति स्थिति के प्रमाण के बिना पात्रता स्थापित नहीं की जा सकती।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Gather appropriate documentation: death certificate of husband (widows), divorce decree (divorced), legal proceedings/police reports/DCW/NCW/FCC/Mahila Panchayat documentation (separated/abandoned/deserted), destitution proof (destitute). Keep originals ready for verification if called.
examples: Widow applicant unable to produce death certificate of husband
Divorced applicant's divorce decree is from an unrecognized court
Separated applicant has no documentation from police, DCW, NCW, FCC, or Mahila Panchayat
Abandoned applicant cannot provide any document establishing abandonment

rejection_rules.description: Submission of forged documents or false statements will result in penal action, monetary penalty, immediate pension stoppage, and recovery of all amounts disbursed. Making false declarations is punishable under relevant Acts and laws.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: जाली दस्तावेज या झूठे बयान जमा करने पर दंडात्मक कार्रवाई, आर्थिक दंड, तत्काल पेंशन रोकना, और वितरित सभी राशियों की वसूली की जाएगी। झूठी घोषणाएं संबंधित अधिनियमों और कानूनों के तहत दंडनीय हैं।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Provide only truthful and accurate information. All documents submitted must be genuine. False income declarations, forged death certificates, or fabricated status proofs will result in severe legal consequences.
examples: Forged death certificate of husband submitted for widow pension
False income self-declaration understating actual income
Fabricated divorce decree or separation documents
Concealing re-marriage status to continue receiving pension

rejection_rules.description: Pension will be permanently stopped if the beneficiary re-marries. The beneficiary is obligated to inform the concerned District WCD Office with a self-declaration upon re-marriage. Failure to inform is punishable.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि लाभार्थी पुनर्विवाह करती है तो पेंशन स्थायी रूप से बंद कर दी जाएगी। लाभार्थी को पुनर्विवाह पर संबंधित जिला महिला एवं बाल विकास कार्यालय को स्व-घोषणा के साथ सूचित करना अनिवार्य है। सूचित न करना दंडनीय है।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Upon re-marriage, immediately inform the concerned District WCD Office with a self-declaration. Concealing re-marriage and continuing to receive pension is punishable with penal action and recovery of amounts.
examples: Beneficiary re-marries but does not inform District Office and continues receiving pension
Re-marriage discovered during periodic home verification visit
Beneficiary conceals re-marriage and is found receiving pension for over a year after

rejection_rules.description: Pension payments will be stopped if Aadhaar is not linked to the bank account via National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI). Additionally, pension will be stopped if the beneficiary is not found at the registered residence during verification, if the address is changed without informing the District Office, or if the bank account is closed or becomes inactive.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आधार राष्ट्रीय भुगतान निगम (एनपीसीआई) के माध्यम से बैंक खाते से नहीं जुड़ा है तो पेंशन भुगतान बंद कर दिया जाएगा। इसके अतिरिक्त, यदि सत्यापन के दौरान लाभार्थी पंजीकृत निवास पर नहीं पाई जाती, पता बिना सूचित किए बदला जाता है, या बैंक खाता बंद/निष्क्रिय हो जाता है तो भी पेंशन रोक दी जाएगी।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Regularly verify Aadhaar-bank linking status at https://uidai.gov.in. If Aadhaar linking shows 'Inactive', contact bank branch to re-activate. Inform District WCD Office of any address change with documentary proof. Keep bank account active with regular transactions.
examples: Aadhaar linking status shows 'Inactive' at NPCI – pension credit fails month after month
Beneficiary moved to a new address in Delhi without informing District Office
Bank account closed by the bank due to inactivity
Beneficiary not found at registered address during home verification visit
